[BUG] No bugs found. Clean implementation.
Could add ETag/Last-Modified headers based on manifest file mtime so Gamma can cache and only refetch when the file changes on disk. Low priority but would reduce API calls during editing.
No bugs found. Clean implementation.
Root cause: module-level import of cut_hls_streamer in cut_routes_media.py crashes router init when file not yet merged to main. Now uses try/except + _require_hls() guard returning 501.
[BUG] None — clean removal
None — clean removal
Permissions were already +x (fixed in the same session before committing scripts/hooks/pre-commit). Only the grep pattern needed fixing.
[BUG] No bugs.
[WORKED] 4 chars/token heuristic is simple but effective enough for warning purposes. Template is currently ~200 tokens — well under budget.
Could count tokens more precisely using tiktoken if available, with len//4 as fallback.
No bugs.

[BUG] Playback (JKL, Space, arrows) kept global intentionally — in Premiere they're panel-scoped but our Source/Program monitors share the same playback state, so scoping would block playback when Eff
[WORKED] The scope guard already existed in the keydown handler (MARKER_FOCUS block at line ~914) — just needed correct scope values. Zero handler changes, pure data change.

Playback (JKL, Space, arrows) kept global intentionally — in Premiere they're panel-scoped but our Source/Program monitors share the same playback state, so scoping would block playback when Effects panel is focused. Needs Source/Program split first.
The hardcoded 3-key pattern was a data loss bug — any new transition field silently dropped. Now uses dict merge with defaults.
[BUG] Main extractor source had fallen behind the actual hover-politsia pack and docs: checked-in code still used complement background and lacked atmospheric_color_depth path, so future reruns could
[WORKED] Syncing directly from the existing cut-engine worktree was clean and low-risk. The diff was focused to one file and regenerated the pack to the exact sidecar truth already documented.
Next 2 hours should go to upstream clean plate QA: compare source vs clean_no_vehicle and clean_no_people region-by-region, quantify residual object energy inside the expected removal zones, and only

Main extractor source had fallen behind the actual hover-politsia pack and docs: checked-in code still used complement background and lacked atmospheric_color_depth path, so future reruns could silently regress canonical output.

[BUG] There are two separate upstream states in play. Fresh default export only emits no-vehicle special-clean for hover-politsia, while stale qwen_gated artifacts still contain historical no-people f

[WORKED] The concrete alias bug was real and fixable locally: special-clean export had been hard-mapped to backgroundRgbaUrl. After adding per-target specialCleanUrls, fresh runtime export emits a tar

Next 2 hours should inspect why qwen-gated hover-politsia no longer propagates cleanVariant/targetPlate for walker into the live export manifest. That is likely the next blocker before judging clean_n

There are two separate upstream states in play. Fresh default export only emits no-vehicle special-clean for hover-politsia, while stale qwen_gated artifacts still contain historical no-people files. Also, current gated rerun did not reproduce plate_06 in the new manifest, so no-people routing remai

layer_extract should have unit tests — currently zero, all bugs found by QA review only

Generate hotkey handler stubs automatically from ALL_ACTIONS list — for any action in the type without a handler entry, emit a console.warn('Unimplemented hotkey: {action}'). Prevents silent no-ops.

The remaining 4 (focusSourceAcquire, runPulseAnalysis, runAutoMontageFavorites, exportTimeline) are t

[BUG] insertGap skips clips that START before playhead but OVERLAP with it — a clip starting at 3s with 5s duration overlaps playhead at 5s but won't shift. FCP7 splits overlapping clips; we skip them
[WORKED] Using existing move_clip ops for insertGap means no new backend op needed — undo stack automatically supports it since applyTimelineOps records individual moves

insertGap skips clips that START before playhead but OVERLAP with it — a clip starting at 3s with 5s duration overlaps playhead at 5s but won't shift. FCP7 splits overlapping clips; we skip them for simplicity.

Add a worktree sync check that warns when files referenced by tasks exist on different branches. Could prevent these cross-branch file-not-found issues.
weather_mediator.py was created on claude/terminal_fafc branch but not merged to agent/iota-mediator, causing file-not-found when working in this worktree. Cross-branch file availability issue.
[WORKED] Existing import block from cut_routes_workers made the fix trivial — one line added.
Consider a static analysis step (grep for undefined names) in CI to catch this class of bug before it reaches worktree.
_resolve_asset_path was used at line 2455 in /media/support endpoint but never imported — would NameError on every call. Function lives in cut_routes_workers.py:1033.

[BUG] -(j.get("created_at") or 0) crashes with TypeError: bad operand type for unary -: 'str' whenever any job exists. ISO8601 strings can't be negated.
[WORKED] Two stable sort passes (created_at DESC, then state_order ASC) is idiomatic Python and preserves intended ordering without type hacks.
Storing created_at as unix float alongside ISO string would make numeric sorts trivial — worth standardizing in cut_mcp_job_store.
-(j.get("created_at") or 0) crashes with TypeError: bad operand type for unary -: 'str' whenever any job exists. ISO8601 strings can't be negated.
[BUG] No additional bugs found. The regression was purely the missing flex:1 on the root div — height:100% is a no-op in a flex column unless the child has a flex allocation.
Could add a DOCKVIEW_LAYOUT_VERSION export so test suites can assert the version number doesn't accidentally regress (someone forgets to bump it after a schema change).
No additional bugs found. The regression was purely the missing flex:1 on the root div — height:100% is a no-op in a flex column unless the child has a flex allocation.

Files lost in squash merge e8fa5a10f. Pattern: squash merges silently drop files.
A one-shot audit of all cross-module function calls vs imports in the routes/ dir would catch all remaining dangling refs before they hit users. Could be a CI grep check.

Identical pattern to tb_1775046771: function used but not imported after module split. cut_routes_workers uses _run_cut_bootstrap_job from cut_routes_bootstrap but the import block only had 3 other functions from that module. Systematic problem — module splits leave dangling references.

Root cause is multi-path: GitCommitTool._auto_complete_tasks calls complete_task (fires _auto_notify), then handler's double-close guard SHOULD catch the second call but may race in edge cases. Dedup at notify level is the robust fix.

git merge-tree output format varies across git versions — the conflict detection heuristic (checking for '<<<<<<' in output) works on git 2.38+ but older merge-tree (pre-2.38) has different output. May need fallback for CI with older git.

[WORKED] reset_event_bus() from event_bus module is the right call — it closes sqlite connections AND clears UDS sockets in one shot, cleaner than manually calling close() on the singleton.
vetka_mcp_bridge.py should get the same reset_event_bus() call in its graceful_shutdown() — it has the same EventBus leak risk.

Original _graceful_shutdown only saved pipeline JSON state — didn't flush EventBus sqlite connections (risk of WAL corruption on hard kill) and didn't call sys.exit(0) so the process could hang after cleanup if stdio was still open.

Dockview uses HTML5 Drag and Drop API (dragstart event). Safari/WebKit WKWebView requires -webkit-user-drag: element CSS for draggable=true to actually fire dragstart. Without it, tabs and void containers are not draggable — panels stuck, only resizable via sash.
